valid.name: always-timestamp-with-time-zone
valid.code: CREATE TABLE t (created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);

errors.message: Use `timestamptz` (or `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`) instead of `timestamp`. `timestamp` is timezone-naive: it stores the wall-clock value you handed in and assumes every reader and writer share the same convention, so two clients on different `TimeZone` settings will disagree on which instant the row represents.

invalid.name: never-timestamptz
invalid.code:
CREATE TABLE t (created_at TIMESTAMPTZ);
CREATE TABLE u (created_at TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE);

errors.message: Use `timestamp` instead of `timestamptz` (or `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`). When the project treats every timestamp as UTC at the application layer, `timestamp` avoids the implicit conversions `timestamptz` performs against each session's `TimeZone` setting.

Use `timestamp` instead of `timestamptz` (or `TIMESTAMP WITH TIME ZONE`). When the project treats every timestamp as UTC at the application layer, `timestamp` avoids the implicit conversions `timestamptz` performs against each session's `TimeZone` setting.
